title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
problem_statement: Find all coordinates where water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans in a height grid.

examples.explanation: These cells can reach both oceans via non-increasing height paths.

intuition: Run two BFS/DFS passes from the ocean borders and intersect reachable cells from both origins.

approaches.name: Bidirectional ocean BFS
approaches.description: Start from Pacific-border cells and Atlantic-border cells separately, then collect cells reachable in both searches.
approaches.why_not: A naive check from each cell would be too slow; reverse reachability is much faster.

interview_qa.q: Why traverse from oceans instead of from each cell?
interview_qa.a: Reverse traversal avoids repeated expensive path searches and naturally enforces non-decreasing height.

interview_qa.q: What condition allows water to flow to a neighbor?
interview_qa.a: Neighbor height must be >= current height when searching backward from the ocean.
